name: Merle Travis
abstract: Merle Robert Travis (November 29, 1917 – October 20, 1983) was an American country and western singer, songwriter, actor, and guitarist. Born in Rosewood, Kentucky, his songs' lyrics were often about the lives and the economic exploitation of American coal miners.

concerts.label: Country Music at Carnegie Hall featuring Lester Flatt and Earl Scruggs, The Foggy Mountain Boys, and Merle Travis

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Spottswood
about_quotes.evidence: Doc Watson named his son Eddie Merle because he was naming him after Eddie Arnold and Merle Travis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Spottswood interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 13, 2023
